workflow.persona: Internal product strategist mapping the workflow used by an outsourced compliance provider
workflow.entry_condition: A draft advertisement or promotional communication is ready for compliance review before publication.
workflow.completion_condition: The item is approved or rejected, the final review package is archived, and monitoring follow-up is queued.
workflow_notes: This first slice models one workflow only.
Representative internal artifacts are included as workflow inputs, not as fabricated evidence.
Source evidence is limited to cited SEC guidance, staff guidance, risk alerts, examination priorities, and enforcement releases.

observed_inputs.note: Source and artifact inventories are observed or curated workflow inputs. Scores and recommendations are inferred from those inputs.

dimensions.label: Automation fit
dimensions.description: Higher means the step is structured, rules-based, and technically repeatable.

dimensions.label: Human sign-off need
dimensions.description: Higher means a named human owner should retain final authority for the step.

dimensions.label: Liability asymmetry
dimensions.description: Higher means the downside of a wrong answer is materially larger than the efficiency gain.

dimensions.label: Evidence strength
dimensions.description: Higher means the step can be anchored in explicit source material, artifacts, and traceable checks.

source_inventory.title: SEC Division of Examinations Announces 2026 Priorities
source_inventory.summary: Signals that advisers should strengthen compliance programs in core areas and be ready for constructive supervisory dialogue in examinations.

source_inventory.title: SEC Charges Nine Investment Advisers in Ongoing Sweep into Marketing Rule Violations
source_inventory.summary: Shows active enforcement for untrue or unsubstantiated statements plus testimonials, endorsements, and third-party ratings that lacked required disclosures.

source_inventory.title: SEC Charges Five Investment Advisers for Marketing Rule Violations
source_inventory.summary: Shows the SEC enforcing hypothetical-performance requirements where advisers advertised to the public without the required policies and procedures.

source_inventory.title: Examinations Focused on the New Investment Adviser Marketing Rule
source_inventory.summary: Explains that examinations will focus on policies and procedures, substantiation, performance advertising, and books and records under the new rule.

source_inventory.title: Examinations Focused on Additional Areas of the Adviser Marketing Rule
source_inventory.summary: Adds testimonials, endorsements, third-party ratings, and Form ADV alignment to the examination focus areas.

source_inventory.title: Marketing Compliance - Frequently Asked Questions
source_inventory.summary: Provides staff views on how the marketing rule applies to nuanced issues such as accompanying information, gross-versus-net presentation, portfolio characteristics, and compliance transition expectations.

source_inventory.title: Initial Observations Regarding Advisers Act Marketing Rule Compliance
source_inventory.summary: Describes observed deficiencies around unsubstantiated statements, omissions, misleading inferences, unbalanced presentations, books and records, and compliance program gaps.

source_inventory.title: Investment Adviser Marketing
source_inventory.summary: Summarizes the marketing rule's general prohibitions plus conditions for testimonials, endorsements, third-party ratings, and performance advertising.

artifact_inventory.name: Approval log and supervisor attestation
artifact_inventory.description: Recorded approval decision, approver identity, date, and any conditions placed on publication.

artifact_inventory.name: Archive bundle and published capture
artifact_inventory.description: Final approved materials, supporting documentation, published screenshot or capture, and retention metadata.

artifact_inventory.name: Channel and audience plan
artifact_inventory.description: Planned distribution channel, intended audience, campaign context, and publication date.

artifact_inventory.name: Campaign brief
artifact_inventory.description: Business objective, target message, and any claims the campaign intends to make.

artifact_inventory.name: Disclosure matrix
artifact_inventory.description: Checklist for required disclosures tied to testimonials, ratings, performance, and other ad claims.

artifact_inventory.name: Draft ad copy
artifact_inventory.description: The proposed text, image, or landing-page copy submitted for review.

artifact_inventory.name: Performance support workbook
artifact_inventory.description: Supporting calculations, assumptions, and intended-audience notes for any performance or hypothetical performance content.

artifact_inventory.name: Compliance review memo
artifact_inventory.description: Issue list, redlines, and remediation asks provided back to marketing or the outsourced provider.

artifact_inventory.name: Substantiation memo
artifact_inventory.description: Short memo linking each material statement of fact to support and noting any unresolved gaps.

artifact_inventory.name: Promoter, testimonial, and third-party rating inventory
artifact_inventory.description: List of testimonials, endorsements, ratings, compensation terms, and conflicts that may trigger disclosures or agreements.

summary.first_build_wedge: Automate the workflow edges, assist the judgment-heavy middle, and keep final publication approval human.

highest_priority_build_step.step_name: Archive the final package and queue monitoring

hard_guardrail_step.step_name: Record principal or CCO approval for publication

lowest_confidence_step.step_name: Draft the review memo and remediation path

summary.blocked_pattern: Do not ship autonomous end-to-end approval or publication for this workflow in slice 1.

review_queue.action: Resolve the listed unknowns before turning this step into stronger product logic.
Resolve the listed unknowns before turning this step into stronger product logic.
Resolve the listed unknowns before turning this step into stronger product logic.

step_results.step_description: Collect the draft content, channel plan, intended audience, attachments, and supporting files into one normalized review packet before any substantive review begins.

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: The work is structured intake and packet assembly with stable required fields.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: A person can review exceptions, but the packet-building step itself does not need final human judgment.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: Errors are usually caught downstream before publication, so the main risk is process friction rather than irreversible harm.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: The intake checklist can be anchored to explicit rule-review inputs, source documents, and books-and-records expectations.

evidence_notes.source_title: Investment Adviser Marketing
evidence_notes.claim: The review starts from identifiable advertisement conditions and content categories, which supports a structured intake packet.

evidence_notes.source_title: Examinations Focused on the New Investment Adviser Marketing Rule
evidence_notes.claim: Examiners review policies, procedures, and books and records, so consistent packet assembly reduces readiness gaps.

evidence_notes.source_title: SEC Division of Examinations Announces 2026 Priorities
evidence_notes.claim: The priorities push firms toward stronger baseline compliance programs, which favors standardized intake over ad hoc email routing.

unknowns: Firm-specific routing fields may vary by channel or outside counsel requirements.
step_results.build_implication: Good automate candidate: normalize intake and create a review-ready bundle before a human reviewer opens the file.

confidence_breakdown.note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Automate is the best fit because automation fit (82) and evidence strength (80) comfortably outweigh human sign-off need (25) and liability asymmetry (30).
decision_rationale.why_not_more_human: Human review should stay focused on exceptions, not default packet handling.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

review_actions: Resolve the listed unknowns before turning this step into stronger product logic.

step_results.step_name: Classify whether the item is an advertisement
step_results.step_description: Determine whether the communication falls inside the rule and whether any exclusions, audience nuances, or channel-specific interpretations affect the review path.

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: The step can follow repeatable rule categories, channel metadata, and content triggers, especially for testimonials, ratings, and performance-like claims.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: A human should still confirm edge cases because classification changes which downstream review controls apply.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: A wrong classification can route the item through the wrong controls, although later review steps can still catch some mistakes before publication.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: The small-entity guide, 2023 exam alert, 2026 FAQ, and 2024 observations provide traceable anchors for common categories and known edge cases.

evidence_notes.source_title: Investment Adviser Marketing
evidence_notes.claim: The guide lays out the rule's prohibited practices and content conditions, which gives a base frame for deciding whether a communication should enter the marketing-rule review path.

evidence_notes.source_title: Examinations Focused on Additional Areas of the Adviser Marketing Rule
evidence_notes.claim: The SEC's additional examination areas for testimonials, endorsements, third-party ratings, and Form ADV alignment create clearer branch points for classifying the communication before deeper review.

evidence_notes.source_title: Marketing Compliance - Frequently Asked Questions
evidence_notes.claim: The updated FAQ says advisers may need to determine whether portfolio or investment characteristics count as performance and emphasizes facts-and-circumstances analysis for certain presentations, which supports assistive classification rather than autonomous routing.

evidence_notes.source_title: Initial Observations Regarding Advisers Act Marketing Rule Compliance
evidence_notes.claim: Observed deficiencies around omissions and misleading inferences show why edge-case classification should still be confirmed by a human reviewer.

unknowns: Some firms treat social fragments, event remarks, or linked content with fact-specific overrides.
step_results.build_implication: Best handled as an assistive classifier that shows why the item likely is or is not an advertisement.

confidence_breakdown.note: Moderate score separation supports the recommendation, but the second-best mode remains plausible.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Assist is the right lane because the step is partially structured (62) but still carries meaningful human sign-off need (69) and liability asymmetry (63).
decision_rationale.why_not_automate: Do not let this step resolve the review on its own; it should prepare judgment, not replace it.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Moderate score separation supports the recommendation, but the second-best mode remains plausible.

review_actions: Resolve the listed unknowns before turning this step into stronger product logic.

step_results.step_name: Flag testimonials, ratings, and performance content
step_results.step_description: Check whether the item includes testimonials, endorsements, third-party ratings, specific investment advice, performance claims, or hypothetical performance that triggers extra conditions.

artifacts.name: Promoter, testimonial, and third-party rating inventory

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: Many triggers are pattern-detectable and checklist-driven, especially for known content types.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: Humans should confirm the detection result because image context, compensation details, and audience fit can change the answer.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: Missing one trigger can create disclosure failures or unsupported performance exposure.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: SEC guidance and enforcement both identify the specific content areas that need extra scrutiny.

evidence_notes.source_title: Investment Adviser Marketing
evidence_notes.claim: The guide spells out conditions for testimonials, endorsements, third-party ratings, and performance information.

evidence_notes.source_title: Examinations Focused on Additional Areas of the Adviser Marketing Rule
evidence_notes.claim: The SEC explicitly elevated testimonials, endorsements, third-party ratings, and Form ADV alignment as focus areas.

evidence_notes.source_title: SEC Charges Nine Investment Advisers in Ongoing Sweep into Marketing Rule Violations
evidence_notes.claim: Recent charges show that missing disclosures and unsubstantiated claims still lead to enforcement.

evidence_notes.source_title: SEC Charges Five Investment Advisers for Marketing Rule Violations
evidence_notes.claim: Hypothetical-performance handling remains an active enforcement topic when audience and policy controls are weak.

unknowns: Creative assets may hide claims inside images or short video captions that require OCR or richer media parsing.
step_results.build_implication: Strong assist candidate: automate red-flag detection and disclosure reminders, but keep human confirmation before any approval.

confidence_breakdown.note: Moderate score separation supports the recommendation, but the second-best mode remains plausible.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Assist is the right lane because the step is partially structured (71) but still carries meaningful human sign-off need (62) and liability asymmetry (68).
decision_rationale.why_not_automate: Do not let this step resolve the review on its own; it should prepare judgment, not replace it.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Moderate score separation supports the recommendation, but the second-best mode remains plausible.

review_actions: Resolve the listed unknowns before turning this step into stronger product logic.

step_results.step_description: Gather backing support for each material statement of fact and build the disclosure package needed for any ratings, testimonials, or performance content.

artifacts.name: Promoter, testimonial, and third-party rating inventory

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: Evidence collection and disclosure assembly can follow structured templates tied to detected claim types.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: Humans still review the final package, but the assembly work itself is mostly operational.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: A bad bundle is still risky, but it is discoverable before publication and can be corrected inside the review loop.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: This step is directly grounded in substantiation and disclosure expectations described in SEC guidance and enforcement.

evidence_notes.source_title: Investment Adviser Marketing
evidence_notes.claim: Advisers need a reasonable basis to substantiate material statements and must satisfy disclosure conditions around specific content types.

evidence_notes.source_title: Initial Observations Regarding Advisers Act Marketing Rule Compliance
evidence_notes.claim: Observed deficiencies included unsubstantiated statements and unbalanced presentations, making evidence bundling a concrete control point.

evidence_notes.source_title: SEC Charges Five Investment Advisers for Marketing Rule Violations
evidence_notes.claim: The SEC has penalized firms for weak hypothetical-performance controls, which increases the value of structured support packages.

evidence_notes.source_title: SEC Charges Nine Investment Advisers in Ongoing Sweep into Marketing Rule Violations
evidence_notes.claim: Enforcement tied to missing disclosures reinforces the need for a repeatable disclosure assembly step.

unknowns: Some firms may require outside-counsel review for unusual performance claims.
step_results.build_implication: High-value automate wedge: package substantiation evidence and disclosure support before human review.

confidence_breakdown.note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Automate is the best fit because automation fit (86) and evidence strength (83) comfortably outweigh human sign-off need (35) and liability asymmetry (45).
decision_rationale.why_not_more_human: Human review should stay focused on exceptions, not default packet handling.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

review_actions: Resolve the listed unknowns before turning this step into stronger product logic.

step_results.step_name: Draft the review memo and remediation path
step_results.step_description: Summarize issues, explain why the item fails or passes, and drive revisions with marketing or outsourced provider teams until the piece is ready for decision.

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: Issue drafting can be scaffolded from recurring deficiency patterns, FAQ-backed issue categories, and artifact checks, even though final phrasing still needs reviewer context.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: A human should still confirm that the proposed explanations and edits actually cure the issue before the item moves to approval.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: Weak remediation guidance can still push a risky ad forward, but this step remains pre-approval and benefits from downstream supervisory review.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: The memo can point to explicit rule conditions, staff FAQ interpretations, observed deficiency themes, and substantiation gaps in a traceable way.

evidence_notes.source_title: Investment Adviser Marketing
evidence_notes.claim: The marketing rule's specific conditions create a reusable frame for issue spotting, so the memo can be structured around recurring categories before a reviewer finalizes the advice.

evidence_notes.source_title: Marketing Compliance - Frequently Asked Questions
evidence_notes.claim: The updated SEC FAQ shows that borderline performance and presentation questions often turn on accompanying information, prominence, and contextual explanation, which fits draft-assist tooling more than autonomous resolution.

evidence_notes.source_title: Initial Observations Regarding Advisers Act Marketing Rule Compliance
evidence_notes.claim: Observed deficiencies around omissions, misleading inferences, and unfairly balanced presentations require nuanced reviewer explanations.

evidence_notes.source_title: SEC Division of Examinations Announces 2026 Priorities
evidence_notes.claim: The SEC expects stronger compliance programs and supervisory readiness, which favors consistent memo structure and remediation tracking but not autonomous dispositioning.

unknowns: Some firms prefer short issue lists while others require formal memo language and legal-style citations.
step_results.build_implication: Assist candidate: draft issue lists, redline suggestions, and remediation checklists, but keep humans in charge of the actual judgment call.

confidence_breakdown.note: Moderate score separation supports the recommendation, but the second-best mode remains plausible.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Assist is the right lane because the step is partially structured (65) but still carries meaningful human sign-off need (75) and liability asymmetry (75).
decision_rationale.why_not_automate: Do not let this step resolve the review on its own; it should prepare judgment, not replace it.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Moderate score separation supports the recommendation, but the second-best mode remains plausible.

review_actions: Resolve the listed unknowns before turning this step into stronger product logic.
Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.

step_results.step_name: Record principal or CCO approval for publication
step_results.step_description: An authorized compliance leader decides whether the piece can be published, records the basis for the decision, and sets any conditions that must hold at launch.

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: This is a supervisory judgment step, not just a checklist action.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: The publication decision should stay with a named human decision-maker.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: A bad automated approval can put a noncompliant ad into market and create direct regulatory exposure.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: There is evidence for why the step matters, but the decision still requires accountable human judgment.

evidence_notes.source_title: SEC Division of Examinations Announces 2026 Priorities
evidence_notes.claim: The examination program emphasizes core compliance readiness, which favors explicit supervisory ownership over autonomous release.

evidence_notes.source_title: SEC Charges Nine Investment Advisers in Ongoing Sweep into Marketing Rule Violations
evidence_notes.claim: Recent enforcement shows real penalties when advertisements go live with missing disclosures or unsupported statements.

evidence_notes.source_title: SEC Charges Five Investment Advisers for Marketing Rule Violations
evidence_notes.claim: Hypothetical-performance violations illustrate how costly a wrong publication decision can become.

unknowns: Some firms require dual sign-off when performance or promoter compensation is involved.
step_results.build_implication: Keep human. Build audit support around this step, not autonomous approval.

confidence_breakdown.note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Keep Human is mandatory because this step is an explicit supervisory approval point.
decision_rationale.why_not_automate: Automation here should only package context and evidence for the accountable reviewer.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

review_actions: Resolve the listed unknowns before turning this step into stronger product logic.
Keep any future UI limited to approval support and audit traceability, not autonomous action.

step_results.step_name: Archive the final package and queue monitoring
step_results.step_description: Store the final approval packet, capture the published output, and queue post-publication monitoring or periodic re-review tasks.

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: Archival packaging and monitoring queue creation are repetitive, structured, and policy-driven.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: Humans should review exceptions, but the default archival path can be system-run.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: The main risk is documentation gaps, which automation can reduce rather than create.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: Books-and-records expectations and recent enforcement make this step highly evidenceable.

evidence_notes.source_title: Examinations Focused on the New Investment Adviser Marketing Rule
evidence_notes.claim: Books and records are a stated exam area, so archive completeness is a concrete control requirement.

evidence_notes.source_title: Initial Observations Regarding Advisers Act Marketing Rule Compliance
evidence_notes.claim: The SEC tied marketing-rule observations back to books-and-records and compliance-rule gaps.

evidence_notes.source_title: SEC Charges Five Investment Advisers for Marketing Rule Violations
evidence_notes.claim: Enforcement actions have included failures to maintain required copies, increasing the value of automated archival capture.

unknowns: Retention schedule and monitoring cadence may vary by firm policy and channel.
step_results.build_implication: Strong automate candidate because it is structured, repetitive, and directly improves exam readiness.

confidence_breakdown.note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Automate is the best fit because automation fit (88) and evidence strength (85) comfortably outweigh human sign-off need (30) and liability asymmetry (38).
decision_rationale.why_not_more_human: Human review should stay focused on exceptions, not default packet handling.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

review_actions: Resolve the listed unknowns before turning this step into stronger product logic.

build_decisions.decision: Build automation around intake, substantiation assembly, and archival packaging before any reviewer-facing UI.
build_decisions.rationale: These steps scored high on automation fit and evidence strength while staying below the configured liability and sign-off gates.

build_decisions.decision: Build human-in-the-loop review aids for classification, content-flagging, and remediation drafting instead of autonomous adjudication.
build_decisions.rationale: These steps benefit from structured guidance, but they still carry meaningful judgment and liability that should stay visible to a reviewer.

build_decisions.decision: Do not build end-to-end autonomous approval or publication in slice 1.
build_decisions.rationale: The publication-approval step remains a named human responsibility with asymmetric downside if a bad ad is cleared.

explicit_defers: No reviewer UI beyond this published payload
